data.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion G6 — approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection): CLEAN / PASS. No findings. Tool-grounded against the shipped eval seam + the three plan-review eval specs.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan satisfies):

- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE — the chosen approach solves the NAMED problem, not a proxy. The "Why" (eval-gate every prompt/criterion change; catch silent descoping of an externally-expressed requirement) maps 1:1 to the mechanism: a standing per-criterion recall/false-accept/false-fire set + ISF cases wired into the gate. The seam is the discipline that actually gates (`validate_eval_spec`/`at_least(k)`/coverage/kappa offline; live Inspect-AI run in the eval CI — `src/rebar/llm/eval.py:191-223,319-351`, `.github/workflows/eval.yml`), and the three specs exist and carry the three case types tagged by corpus (`plan-review-finder.eval.yaml` recall R-* / false-fire FP1-FP7; `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`; `plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml`).

- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (data-flow complete; the "suppress FPs WITHOUT trading for false negatives" claim is actually realized, not just asserted). The load-bearing risk in this plan is the suppression-vs-recall trade; it is NOT left as a vibe — the verifier spec implements paired `{true, false}` discrimination cases (D1/D2/D3) with a deterministic `discriminates_true_from_false` GATING scorer (planted-false validity STRICTLY LOWER, no over-suppression of true findings) AND an explicit `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` deterministic scorer (true high-impact finding's graded validity stays ≥ 0.5) — exactly the inverse false-NEGATIVE axis the Scope demands. The FP6-vs-T3 boundary case (drop library-CONTRACT re-proof D3-false; KEEP library-FEATURE-maturity D3-true) shows the trade is held at case granularity, not blanket-suppressed.

- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (T12 discrimination edge handled). The added T12 AC is realized as a genuine discrimination PAIR, not a one-sided suppression: R-T12-snap (deployed runtime, no staged-rollout/rollback → SHOULD fire, recall) vs T12-suppress-rebar (git-backed library/CLI → not-applicable). This is the correct way to validate a criterion for discrimination rather than only its calibration-history suppression.

- APPROACH SELECTION (defensible; generated alternatives discarded). I generated two structurally-different alternatives: (a) a bespoke standalone eval harness instead of reusing the shipped Inspect-AI seam, and (b) folding ISF's eval cases into 681b rather than a clean owner-split. The plan picks the shipped seam (lowest blast-radius, best codebase-alignment, deterministic-gates-only discipline already in place) and pins an explicit ownership split (`681b` owns the ISF criterion+prompt+gating; `7284` owns the suite/harness + labeled cases, via the `relates_to` link) that avoids duplicate-build drift. The chosen approach is at-least-as-good on alignment/blast-radius/testability/simplicity — alternatives discarded, no coaching finding.

- POSITIVE RATIONALE present. The "Why" states it ("must be eval-gated, not vibes; the gate currently cannot catch silent descoping"), and Scope/What give the seam-reuse + ownership-split rationale. G6(4) satisfied without a (anti-priming) rejected-alternatives section.

Note (not a G6 finding — adjacent, for awareness): the plan's What names the spec path as `.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml` while the shipped specs are packaged under `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` (a project `.rebar/evals/<id>` override wins over the packaged copy per `load_eval_spec`, eval.py:69-90, and README.plan-review.md). This is a documentation/locality nuance, not an approach-soundness defect — the mechanism is correct either way — so no G6 finding.
